revision.text:
[[en:Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]][[fa:هاریما نو اینابی نو اوایراتسومه]][[ja:播磨稲日大郎姫]][[zh:播磨稻日大郎姬]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0122|6|25}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Keikō|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}|children={{ill|Prince Kamukushi|qid=Q11590432|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Oousu|qid=Q11438297|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Wakoneko|qid=Q110449883|1=Wakoneko}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}|father={{ill|Prince Wakatakehiko|qid=Q11596350|1=Wakatakehiko}}|native_name=印南別嬢; 播磨稲日大郎姫; 針間之伊那毘能大郎女}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume | consort_reason = Empress of the 12th Emperor | image = | image_width = | alt_text = | caption = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, 3rd month, 3rd day | senior_Empress = | Grand Empress = | Great Empress = | Deposed = | Court title = | other_titles = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = 52nd year of Emperor Keikō, 5th month, 4th day | death_place = | funeral = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | naming_date = | former_name = | alternate_titles = | other_names = | childhood_name = | seal = | clan = [[Kibi clan|Kibi no Omi]]/{{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|qid=Q11417873|lt=Marube no Omi|1=Wani clan}} | father = {{ill|Wakatakehiko|ja|稚武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakatakehiko|qq=}} | mother = | spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} | spouse2 = | spouse3 = | entered_palace = | marriage = | children = Kushitsuno-wake no Ō
[[Ōusu no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} (Ōusu no Mikoto)
Yamato-neko no Mikoto
{{ill|Kamukushi no Mikoto|ja|神櫛皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamukushi no Mikoto|qq=}} | foster_children = | court_titles = | Rank_before_empress = | honors = | positions = | autograph = | notes = }} '''Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume''' (播磨稲日大郎姫, birth date unknown – 52nd year of Emperor Keikō, 5th month, 4th day) was the {{ill|Empress|en|Empress|qid=Q100387742|lt=Empress|1=Empress}} of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and the mother of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}. She is also known as Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume and Inami no Waki-iratsume. == Records == === Kojiki ===
'''Genealogy based on the Kojiki''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|~|~|~|02|~|~|~|~|~|y|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|Inami no Waka-iratsume}}|02={{Supra|12}}{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}|03={{color|#FC4E6B|'''Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume'''}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.|||||,|-|v|-|-|v|^|-|v|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||02||03|04|05|06|07| 01=Makuwaka no Ō|02=[[Hiko-hito no Ō]]|03=Kushitsuno-wake no Ō|04=[[Ōusu no Mikoto]]|05={{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}|06=Yamato-neko no Mikoto|07={{ill|Kamukushi no Mikoto|ja|神櫛皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}}} {{familytree/end}}
Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume appears in the ''Kojiki'' as "Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume" or "Inabi no Waka-iratsume." She is described as the daughter of [[Wakatake Kibi-tsu-hiko]], who was the son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}. She is listed as the mother of princes such as Kushitsuno-wake no Ō, [[Ōusu no Mikoto]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, Yamato-neko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Kamukushi no Mikoto|ja|神櫛皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamukushi no Mikoto|qq=}}. Her sister, Inabi no Waka-iratsume, is also described as one of Emperor Keikō’s consorts. === Nihon Shoki ===
'''Genealogy based on the Nihon Shoki''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|~|~|~|y|~|~|~|~|02||01={{Supra|12}}{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}|02={{color|#FC4E6B|'''Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume
(Inabi no Waka-iratsume)'''}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||,|-|-|^|-|-|.|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01||||02| 01=[[Ōusu no Mikoto]]|02={{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}}} {{familytree/end}}
In the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume is identified as Empress and is said to have been formally installed on the 3rd day of the 3rd month of Emperor Keikō's 2nd year. She is credited as the mother of [[Ōusu no Mikoto]] and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}. Some sources suggest she is also the mother of Waka Yamato-neko no Mikoto. Her name is alternately written as "Inabi no Waka-iratsume," which corresponds to her sister described in the ''Kojiki.'' She passed away in the 52nd year of Emperor Keikō’s reign. === Harima no Kuni Fudoki === In the ''{{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}'' (Topography of Harima Province), she appears as Inami no Waki-iratsume. The ''Fudoki'' describes her as the daughter of Hiko Namuchi (ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|qid=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}}) and Kibi-hime. Emperor Keikō pursued her in Harima, where she initially hid, but the emperor eventually found her. They were united at the castle palace (possibly in the present-day Kakonohama area). After her death, her tomb was constructed at [[:en:Hioka|Hioka]]. However, when transporting her remains, her body sank into the river, and only her comb and thin shawl were buried instead. The genealogical connection between Hiko Namuchi and Inami no Waki-iratsume is debated as Hiko Namuchi was contemporaneous with Emperor Seimu, raising chronological questions. == Burial == [[File:Hiokaryo Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|200px|{{center|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume's [[Hinooka Kofun|Hioka Kofun]]
({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, [[Kakogawa City]])}}]] Her burial site, designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, is identified as the '''Hioka Kofun''' (日岡陵) in [[Kakogawa City]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun approximately 80 meters in length. {{Consorts of Japan}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{Consorts of Japan}}]] {{translated page|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|lt=Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|version=96457668|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume]] [[ja:播磨稲日大郎姫]] [[zh:播磨稻日大郎姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11078587}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
